title: Permutation-invariant field-context model for same-lap competitors
summary: Whether a driver pits depends partly on the whole field state: nearby rivals, compound mix, and lap-wide stop pressure.
rationale: Whether a driver pits depends partly on the whole field state: nearby rivals, compound mix, and lap-wide stop pressure. Deep Sets and Set Transformer are designed for set-valued inputs where order should not matter, which matches the per-lap roster much better than hand-made aggregates alone.
implementation_hint: Build one expert where each sample is the focal driver plus the set of all other drivers in the same `Year/Race/LapNumber`; encode each competitor row, pool with Deep Sets or a small Set Transformer, and output a pit probability or rank score. Use OOF predictions only for stacking with the tabular ensemble.
expected_effect: Adds interaction signal beyond current averages/top-k pressure features and may help especially on chaotic laps where handcrafted summaries miss which combination of cars is creating the stop window.
risk: More engineering and tuning work than tree models; benefits may be modest if the tabular features already summarize most field context.
